scope: geophysical, piezoelectric, fracture, atmospheric-electric and observational mechanism registry

sources.title: Gold nugget formation from earthquake-induced piezoelectricity in quartz
supports: stressed quartz can generate electrochemical conditions that deposit dissolved gold under tested laboratory and model conditions
does_not_support: elemental transmutation
gold creation by squeezing dry quartz
universal gold-vein formation mechanism
sources.safe_use: Experimental anchor for a conditional quartz-piezoelectric electrochemical pathway.

sources.title: Numerical Simulation of Electromagnetic Responses to an Earthquake Source Due To the Piezoelectric Effect
sources.journal_or_authority: Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth
supports: semi-analytical prediction of early and coseismic electromagnetic responses from quartz-rich piezoelectric rock models
does_not_support: universal earthquake precursor
observed validation for every earthquake
sources.safe_use: Model comparator for expected field scale, timing and conductivity dependence.

sources.title: Correlation between nanosecond X-ray flashes and stick-slip friction in peeling tape
supports: radio, visible and nanosecond X-ray emission from peeling adhesive tape in moderate vacuum
does_not_support: equivalent X-ray output during ordinary tape use in air
static electricity as a complete universal explanation
sources.safe_use: Specific tribological high-energy emission benchmark with vacuum and timing controls.

sources.title: Electromagnetic emission accompanying fracture of quartz-bearing rocks
supports: laboratory RF transients from quartz and tourmaline fracture
does_not_support: unique attribution of all rock-fracture emissions to quartz
field-scale earthquake prediction
sources.safe_use: Historical laboratory benchmark for fracture-linked RF transients.

sources.title: Radio emission associated with rock fracture: Possible application to the Great Chilean Earthquake of May 22, 1960
sources.journal_or_authority: Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth
supports: laboratory radio emission during microfracture in granite
piezoelectric field model as a candidate mechanism
does_not_support: confirmed universal precursor status
exclusive causal attribution of the 1960 radio event
sources.safe_use: Candidate mechanism and historical case, to be used with competing controls.

sources.title: Sources of electromagnetic radiation from fracture of rock samples in the laboratory
supports: electrical and optical emissions during rock fracture
does_not_support: quartz piezoelectricity as the sole or necessarily dominant fracture-emission mechanism

sources.title: Earthquake lights and the stress-activation of positive hole charge carriers in rocks
sources.journal_or_authority: Physics and Chemistry of the Earth / USGS record
supports: positive-hole charge carriers as a candidate mechanism for luminous and high-frequency emissions from stressed rock
does_not_support: confirmed explanation for every earthquake light
operational earthquake prediction

sources.title: Electromagnetic fields generated by earthquakes
supports: coseismic electromagnetic effects and multiple candidate mechanisms
need for broad spectral and instrumental analysis
does_not_support: clear demonstrated general precursory electromagnetic signal
sources.safe_use: Review-level claim boundary and mechanism plurality anchor.

sources.title: Synopsis of the historical development of Schumann resonances
supports: Earth-ionosphere cavity ELF resonance framework and atmospheric electrical excitation
does_not_support: direct conversion of solar wind into Schumann resonance
local geological control of global cavity eigenfrequencies
sources.safe_use: Reference model for the cavity and source taxonomy.

sources.title: Modifications of Middle Atmosphere Conductivity During Sudden Ionospheric Disturbances Deduced From Changes of Schumann Resonance Peak Frequencies
supports: solar X-ray flare modulation of ionospheric height and Schumann peak frequencies through conductivity changes
does_not_support: solar wind as the ordinary direct source of Schumann modes

sources.title: Full 3-D TLM simulations of the Earth-ionosphere cavity: Effect of conductivity on the Schumann resonances
sources.journal_or_authority: Journal of Geophysical Research: Space Physics
does_not_support: single homogeneous cavity as sufficient for all observations
sources.safe_use: Numerical baseline for cavity conductivity and geometry.

sources.title: Satellite observations of Schumann resonances in the Earth's ionosphere
supports: Schumann signatures observed at satellite altitude and a leaky-cavity interpretation
does_not_support: unbounded or lossless cavity
sources.safe_use: Observation anchor for propagation beyond the nominal lower-ionosphere boundary.

sources.title: Impact of Solar Activity on Schumann Resonance: Model and Experiment
supports: long-baseline assessment of solar-activity modulation of ionospheric conductivity and Schumann observations
does_not_support: identity between geomagnetic pulsations and lightning-driven cavity modes
sources.safe_use: Recent model-observation bridge for solar modulation.
